title: Fractional Quantum Hall Topological Order Is Not Yet Curated
summary: Witten Lecture Three's Wilson-loop algebra, topological ground-state degeneracy, and quasiparticle-statistics branch is too wide to promote in one unit; it is therefore parked in the future buffer and split into narrower sub-gaps.
assumptions: The current implementation pass is prioritizing the integer Hall / edge-mode flagship chains.
The fractional Hall branch requires a wider background-source expansion before it can be treated as proof-grade.
scope: Deferred topological-order branch covering Wilson loops, torus degeneracy, and fractionalized quasiparticle data.

source_anchors.notes: This is the broad Lecture Three branch that is explicitly deferred to the future buffer.

representation: Future-buffer gap for a branch that is known to matter but not yet narrow enough for current proof-grade promotion.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query reaches Lecture Three topological order and the KB needs to answer honestly that the branch is intentionally buffered.
